name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 03.11.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 03.11.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi  Sowing  Status: Rabi  sowing  data  collection  has  been  started  and  the  sowing  is  in preliminary stage. As on October 31, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 75.77 lakh ha (including 41.69 lakh  ha in Rapeseed & Mustard) as compared to 65.88 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi  Sowing  Status: Rabi  sowing  data  collection  has  been  started  and  the  sowing  is  in preliminary stage. As on October 31, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 75.77 lakh ha (including 41.69 lakh  ha in Rapeseed & Mustard) as compared to 65.88 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · As on 31.10.2025,  37.79%  of  area  has  been  harvested  all  over  India.  Approximately  59.83% area of Pulses mainly in Urdbean (64%), Moongbean (93%) & Mothbean (100%); 55.71% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (54.48%) & Sesamum (60.09%); 59.54% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (67.54%)  & Bajra (86.53%) and 28% area of Rice has been harvested for kharif crops 2025-26.
texts.text: As on 31.10.2025,  37.79%  of  area  has  been  harvested  all  over  India.  Approximately  59.83% area of Pulses mainly in Urdbean (64%), Moongbean (93%) & Mothbean (100%); 55.71% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (54.48%) & Sesamum (60.09%); 59.54% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (67.54%)  & Bajra (86.53%) and 28% area of Rice has been harvested for kharif crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum temperature is likely to be above normal over North East India, East India, Central India, Gujarat and many parts of North West India.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum temperature is likely to be above normal over North East India, East India, Central India, Gujarat and many parts of North West India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01 -November  02  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  49%  higher  than  its  Long  Period Average  (LPA),  with regional variations:  (i) East  &  Northeast  India:  20%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  158%;  (iii)  Central  India:  71%;  and South Peninsula: 32%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01 -November  02  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  49%  higher  than  its  Long  Period Average  (LPA),  with regional variations:  (i) East  &  Northeast  India:  20%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  158%;  (iii)  Central  India:  71%;  and South Peninsula: 32%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to influence Western Himalayan region from 4th November, 2025.
texts.text: A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to influence Western Himalayan region from 4th November, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Formation of a low-pressure area over eastcentral Bay of Bengal & adjoining Myanmar coast, under its influence heavy rainfall at isolated places likely over Andaman & Nicobar Islands today, the 02 nd  November, 2025.
texts.text: Formation of a low-pressure area over eastcentral Bay of Bengal & adjoining Myanmar coast, under its influence heavy rainfall at isolated places likely over Andaman & Nicobar Islands today, the 02 nd  November, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Above normal rainfall is likely in East India, NE India, Gujarat, East Rajasthan, West M.P., Vidarbha and U.P. Below normal rainfall is likely in South India.
texts.text: Above normal rainfall is likely in East India, NE India, Gujarat, East Rajasthan, West M.P., Vidarbha and U.P. Below normal rainfall is likely in South India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated to scattered rainfall activity likely over Eastern and Southern India.
texts.text: Isolated to scattered rainfall activity likely over Eastern and Southern India.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water Reservoir Status : As on 30 October 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 165.782 billion cubic meter, which is 90.85% of total live storage capacity (182.479 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 104.4 percent of last year's storage and 116.3 percent of the average storage of the last ten years  during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better  than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status : As on 30 October 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 165.782 billion cubic meter, which is 90.85% of total live storage capacity (182.479 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 104.4 percent of last year's storage and 116.3 percent of the average storage of the last ten years  during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better  than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text: The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.
texts.text: Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.
texts.text: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Paddy  (Bacterial  leaf  blight  Brown  plant  hopper  and  False Smut)  in  Jharkhand.  The  Advisory  has  been  issued  by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Paddy  (Bacterial  leaf  blight  Brown  plant  hopper  and  False Smut)  in  Jharkhand.  The  Advisory  has  been  issued  by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.orig: · During the week (23 Oct -29 Oct 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 158%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 29 Oct 2025) is higher than LPA by 39%.
texts.text: During the week (23 Oct -29 Oct 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 158%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 29 Oct 2025) is higher than LPA by 39%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture as on 29 Oct 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand,  Odisha, Haryana, Punjab, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P. and Bihar.
texts.text: Soil moisture as on 29 Oct 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand,  Odisha, Haryana, Punjab, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P. and Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It  was  lower  than  average  of  past  9  years  in  pockets  of  Bihar , in  North  eastern  parts  of Jharkhand , in pockets of eastern part of UP and in pockets of Assam and West Bengal.
texts.text: It  was  lower  than  average  of  past  9  years  in  pockets  of  Bihar , in  North  eastern  parts  of Jharkhand , in pockets of eastern part of UP and in pockets of Assam and West Bengal.
